title: Aetheolaena involucrata
intro: Aetheolaena involucrata, commonly known as 'Palo de Sombrero', is a plant species native to the Andean region, belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant is valued for its traditional use in folk medicine, particularly for its potential health benefits in treating various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Aetheolaena involucrata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Aetheolaena involucrata include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of fever and digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Aetheolaena involucrata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The genus Aetheolaena is a group of flowering plants that are native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Aetheolaena involucrata is a specific species within this genus. The plant is further classified as a member of the subfamily Carduoideae. It is a part of the larger kingdom Plantae, which includes all types of plants.

Its medicinal uses include treating various health issues, such as digestive problems and inflammation.
